items.question: ___ the sun goes down, it gets cold here. (certain)
explanations: Correct! The sun definitely goes down, so 'when'.
'Whether' introduces a choice, not this time clause.

items.question: ___ it rains tomorrow, we'll cancel the picnic. (uncertain)
I'll call you ___ I get to the airport. (certain arrival)
explanations: Correct! You will arrive, so 'when I get to the airport'.
'If' would suggest you might not arrive.

items.question: ___ you need anything, just let me know. (possible)
explanations: Correct! Needing something is a possibility, so 'if'.
'When' would suggest it's certain you'll need something.
'As soon as' implies it will happen.

items.question: Choose the present simple: 'When it ___ , I'll open the window.'
explanations: Correct! Use the present simple after 'when': 'when it gets hot'.
The past simple doesn't fit a future 'when'.

items.question: We'll move to a bigger house ___ the baby is born. (certain)
explanations: Correct! The baby will be born, so 'when'.
'If' would suggest doubt about the birth.
'In case' is a precaution, not this time clause.

items.question: ___ I win the competition, I'll be amazed. (unlikely)
explanations: 'As soon as' treats it as certain.

items.question: ___ I drink coffee late, I can't sleep. (a general pattern)
Correct this: 'When I will finish work, I'll come over.'
options: When I finish work, I'll come over.
When I will finish work, I'll come over.
If I will finish work, I'll come over.
When I finishing work, I'll come over.
explanations: Correct! Use the present simple after 'when': 'When I finish work'.
'If I will' is also wrong, and 'if' changes the meaning.
'When I finishing' uses the wrong form.

items.question: Which word shows the event is CERTAIN, only the time is unknown?
explanations: Correct! 'When' = it will happen; only the timing is open.
'If' shows a possibility that may not happen.
